query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Rapine', 'The seizure of one''s property by force, plunder', 'Scholars noted a striking rapine between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rapport', 'A relationship of mutual trust and respect', 'Scholars noted a striking rapport between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rapt', 'Very interested, involved in something, absorbed', 'The reviewer described the tone as rapt, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raptorial', 'Preying upon other animals', 'Scholars noted a striking raptorial between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rapture', 'To experience great happiness or excitement', 'Over time, the community learned to rapture its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rash', 'Hasty, not careful or considered', 'Scholars noted a striking rash between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rasp', 'To scrape or abrade with a rough instrument', 'Critics argued that the plan might rasp the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ratify', 'Formally confirm or approve', 'The author hoped the new policy would ratify tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ration', 'A fixed allowance of provisions or food', 'The essay highlights the ration of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rationale', 'A set of reasons or a logical basis for a course of action', 'Her rationale became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raucous', 'Harsh', 'The passage presents a raucous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ravage', 'To devastate or destroy something', 'Critics argued that the plan might ravage the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ravel', 'To pull apart (especially cloth or a seam), unravel', 'Over time, the community learned to ravel its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ravenous', 'Very hungry', 'The reviewer described the tone as ravenous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ravine', 'A deep narrow valley in the earth''s surface caused by water', 'The essay highlights the ravine of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raze', 'To demolish, to level to the ground', 'The author hoped the new policy would raze tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Readily', 'Promptly', 'She spoke readily, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Readjust', 'Adjust again', 'The essay highlights the readjust of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reaffirm', 'To confirm or assert again', '“The president reaffirmed his commitment to the policy.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Realism', 'A concern for fact and rejection of the impractical', 'Her realism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Realm', 'An abstract sphere of influence, real or imagined', 'The essay highlights the realm of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reaper', 'A machine for cutting standing grain', 'Her reaper became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rearrange', 'To change the order or arrangement of (one or more items)', 'Critics argued that the plan might rearrange the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reassure', 'To restore confidence to', 'The author hoped the new policy would reassure tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rebellious', 'Defying or resisting some established authority', 'The passage presents a rebellious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rebuff', 'Reject bluntly', 'Her rebuff became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rebuild', 'To build again', 'Over time, the community learned to rebuild its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rebuke', 'Express sharp disapproval', 'Scholars noted a striking rebuke between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rebut', 'To refute by evidence or argument', 'The author hoped the new policy would rebut tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rebuttal', 'A response that contradicts or refutes an argument', '“His rebuttal effectively countered the opposing viewpoint.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recalcitrant', 'Resistant to authority or control', '“The recalcitrant student refused to follow the teacher’s instructions.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recant', 'To say that one no longer holds an opinion or belief', 'The author hoped the new policy would recant tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recapitulate', 'To summarize or repeat in concise form', 'The author hoped the new policy would recapitulate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recapture', 'To capture something for a second time', 'The author hoped the new policy would recapture tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recede', 'To move back, to move away', 'Over time, the community learned to recede its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Receivable', 'Fit for acceptance', 'What seemed receivable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Receptacle', 'A container', 'The essay highlights the receptacle of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Receptive', 'Capable of receiving something', 'The reviewer described the tone as receptive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recession', 'The act or an instance of receding', 'The essay highlights the recession of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recessive', 'Going back, receding', 'What seemed recessive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recidivism', 'Repeated or habitual relapse', 'Scholars noted a striking recidivism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recidivist', 'A repeat offender', 'Her recidivist became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reciprocal', 'Given, felt, or done in return', '“Their relationship was built on reciprocal trust and respect.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reciprocate', 'To give or feel in return', 'The author hoped the new policy would reciprocate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reciprocity', 'Mutual exchange of benefits or privileges', '“The trade agreement was based on reciprocity between the two nations.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recitation', 'The act of publicly reciting something previously memorized', 'Scholars noted a striking recitation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reck', 'To care for, to heed, to regard, consider', 'Over time, the community learned to reck its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reckless', 'Careless or heedless, headstrong or rash', 'Her reckless became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reclaim', 'To demand the return or restoration of something as a right', 'Critics argued that the plan might reclaim the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Recline', 'To cause to lean back, to bend back', 'The author hoped the new policy would recline tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
